title: Moments and bounds for L-functions of large degree
speaker: Paul Nelson
abstract: We will discuss recent results concerning the problem of establishing rigorous moment estimates and subconvex bounds for L-functions of large degree.
tags: 50 Years of Number Theory and Random Matrix Theory Conference

proof_stages.label: Moment notation and primary-range heuristic
proof_stages.summary: The talk fixes a large conductor parameter q, families of automorphic forms, 2k-th moments and cuspidal variants, then explains via the approximate functional equation why sufficiently large and complete families should detect only the diagonal in a primary range.

proof_stages.label: Large-degree moment landscape
proof_stages.summary: A sequence of increasingly specialized automorphic families is compared by conductor, family size and known moment results, motivating large-degree L-functions as a setting where higher moments may be within rigorous reach but remain largely open.

proof_stages.label: Short spectral families and subconvexity target
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines short windows for GLD spectral parameters, identifies the threshold partial-sum moments arising from the approximate functional equation, surveys known low-rank cases, and states the resulting general subconvex saving of size roughly 1/(12d^5).

proof_stages.label: Rankin-Selberg period identity
proof_stages.summary: Rankin-Selberg integral representations for GL(d+1) x GL(d) are introduced as the analytic framework expressing L-values as periods of automorphic forms times local Whittaker integrals, enabling averages through spectral or pretrace identities.

proof_stages.label: Microlocal vectors from coadjoint orbits
proof_stages.summary: For a coadjoint parameter tau, the speaker constructs approximate eigenvectors and projector-like operators omega_tau that behave like rank-one idempotents, select short families, and make local zeta integrals non-negligible precisely under a restriction-matching condition.

proof_stages.label: Kernel and Eisenstein wave-packet reduction
proof_stages.summary: An automorphic kernel built from omega_tau has a spectral expansion collapsing to the desired short family, and replacing the smaller representation by Eisenstein wave packets turns the period formula into a geometric expression for dyadic approximate-functional-equation proxies for moments.

proof_stages.label: Geometric off-diagonal estimates
proof_stages.summary: The proof of the subconvex estimate is reduced to a linear-algebra nonvanishing lemma giving transversality and a bilinear-form saving, together with local L2 growth bounds for Eisenstein wave packets that control the noncompact and unipotent contributions.

proof_stages.label: Shifted-moment asymptotics outlook
proof_stages.summary: For full shifted moments with genuine Eisenstein series, the speaker compares the geometric expansion with recipe-predicted main terms, notes that mirabolic sums give only two terms, and proposes further matrix orbits and other integral representations as the source of the remaining terms.
